content.full_text:
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance/Administration       •       Executive/Legal       •       Enforcement       •       Conflicts of Interest
(916) 322-5660                                322-5901                    322-4441                323-6444

July 10, 1978

Mr. Allen Grimes
City Attorney's Office
Post Office Box 321
San Luis Obispo, CA 93406

Dear Mr. Grimes:

This letter is in response to your letter of June 19, 1978,
requesting advice with respect to the conflict of interest
provisions of the Political Reform Act.

I have reviewed your letter to Mr. Henry Engen and generally
agree with its content. However, there is one additional section
of which you should be aware. Commission Regulation 2 Cal.
Adm. Code Section 18700(e)(2) provides that a public official
is attempting to use his official position to influence a
governmental decision when he appears before an agency over
which his agency has budgetary control if the decision in
question will foreseeably and materially affect his financial
interests. Therefore, a commission member is prohibited from
representing clients before an agency over which his commission
has budgetary control.

In this regard, you also may be interested in Government Code
Section 1126.

If I may be of further assistance, please do not hesitate to
contact me.

Sincerely,

Ted Prim
Chief, Conflicts of
Interest Division

TP:mfa

city of san luis OBISPO
CITY ATTORNEY'S OFFICE
Post Office Box 321 • San Luis Obispo, CA 93406 • 805/541-[illegible]

June 19, 1978

Michael Bennett
Executive Director
Fair Political Practices Commission
1100 K Street Bldg.
Sacramento, CA 95814

Dear Sir:

I would like to request an informal opinion on the question that
is presented in the enclosed opinion.

Your assistance is appreciated.

Sincerely,

WENDT, MITCHELL, SINSHEIMER,
de la MOTTE & LILLEY
City Attorney

[signature]

By Allen Grimes

AG:ktm

enc.

city of san luis obispo
CITY ATTORNEY'S OFFICE
Post Office Box 321 • San Luis Obispo, CA 93406 • [illegible] JUN 21-10:35 AM '78

June 19, 1978

MEMORANDUM OPINION NO. 78-15

Requested by: Henry Engen, Community Development Director

Subject : Conflict of Interest

I have your request for an opinion in the conflict of interest field. The
basis of the inquiry is whether there is a conflict of interest when one member
of a city commission, who is a professional person, represents an applicant
who desires the approval of another city commission. Because there are three
members of the planning commission who are engaged in occupations which may
require them to apply to the ARC for action favorable to their occupations,
it may create a situation where members of the commission may reciprocally
judge the merits of projects represented by other commissioners.

There is no legal impediment for a member of a commission who is a professional
person from appearing before a local agency as a representative of a person
seeking the approval of a project by the committee. This is true even though
he knows the members of the local agency considering the matter.

However, the rule is different if the matter is being considered by the local
agency of which he is a member and he has received at least $250 from his
client within the preceding twelve month period. In that case he must disqualify himself, state the nature of the conflict, step down and decline to
participate in discussions with members of the agency and in fact, not participate in any way in the decision of the matter.

The exclusion does not apply, however, if the member of the local agency is
appearing before his agency solely to represent himself. This has complex
ramifications and I am seeking an informal opinion of the staff of the Fair
Political Practices Commission on the matter.

Respectfully submitted,

WENDT, MITCHELL, SINSHEIMER,
de la MOTTE & LILLEY
City Attorney

By Allen Grimes

AG:ktm
